REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE COMPLAINT ASSERTS 4 CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON TWO ALTERNATIVES (THE FIRST TWO CAUSES OF ACTION SEEK DETERMINATION OF RIGHTS AND REMEDIES IF ALL CO-OWNERS ARE BOUND BY TICA; THE SECOND TWO CAUSES OF ACTION APPEAR TO SEEK DETERMINATION OF RIGHTS AND REMEDIES IF THERE IS NO AGREEMENT IN PLACE. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST TWO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND BREACH OF CONTRACT IS OVERRULED. MOVING PARTY DOES NOT CITE ANY AUTHORITY DIRECTLY ON POINT ADDRESSING THE IMPACT OF A FORECLOSURE SALE OF PARTIAL INTERESTS IN PROPERTIES THAT ARE SUBJECT TO TIC AGREEMENTS. SAIN V. SILVESTRE (1978) 78 CAL.APP.3D 461 IS FACTUALLY DISTINGUISHABLE. THE DEMURRER TO THE NEXT TWO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND ACCOUNTING IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE A VIABLE CAUSE OF ACTION AS BETWEEN TENANTS-IN-COMMON IN THE ABSENCE OF A TIC AGREEMENT. BASED ON THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT AND ARGUMENTS IN THE OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF BELIEVES THAT IN THE ABSENCE OF A BINDING TICA AMONG ALL CO-OWNERS, PLAINTIFF AND THE OTHER SIGNATORY TO THE TICA ARE ENTITLED TO ITS BENEFITS (I.E. EXCLUSIVE POSSESSION OF "THEIR" UNITS) WHILE ALL CO-OWNERS HAVE A RIGHT TO JOINT POSSESSION OF DEFENDANT'S (NON-SIGNATORY) UNIT. PLAINTIFF CITES NO AUTHORITY TO SUPPORT THIS THEORY. IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO SEEK DETERMINATION OF THE PARTIES RIGHTS AND REMEDIES IN THE COMPLETE ABSENCE OF THE TIC AGREEMENT, PLAINTIFF MAY ASSERT SUCH CLAIMS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The complaint asserts 4 causes of action based on two alternatives (the first two causes of action seek determination of rights and remedies IF all co-owners are bound by TICA; the second two causes of action appear to seek determination of rights and remedies if there is no agreement in place.
The demurrer to the first two causes of action for declaratory relief and breach of contract is OVERRULED. Moving party does not cite any authority directly on point addressing the impact of a foreclosure sale of partial interests in properties that are subject to TIC agreements. Sain v. Silvestre (1978) 78 Cal.App.3d 461 is factually distinguishable.
The demurrer to the next two causes of action for declaratory relief and accounting is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a viable cause of action as between tenants-in-common in the absence of a TIC agreement. Based on the allegations in the complaint and arguments in the opposition to the demurrer it appears that Plaintiff believes that in the absence of a binding TICA among all co-owners, Plaintiff and the other signatory to the TICA are entitled to its benefits (i.e. exclusive possession of "their" units) while all co-owners have a right to joint possession of Defendant's (non-signatory) unit. Plaintiff cites no authority to support this theory. If Plaintiff wishes to seek determination of the parties rights and remedies in the complete absence of the TIC agreement, Plaintiff may assert such claims.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
